programmes.show_description: Sketch helps Sian cover a clown tattoo that's scaring the life out of anyone who sees it. Jay has to deal with Emma's extremely intimate cat portrait.
Charlotte Ritchie, Jamali Maddix, Lee Mack, Mike Wozniak and Sarah Kendall catch rats and have a smashing time delivering crockery on a scooter.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Michelle Wolf take on Jon Richardson and Rob Beckett. Spencer Jones joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. Rachel Riley looks after the numbers.
Teleshopping
Teleshopping
In Stoke-on-Trent Phil's a BBQ pro. Can the newbie hosts find their flow? Gary and Simon attempt to do prawn. But it's the third time this week, will the guests simply yawn? #WorldPoetryDay
Who'll walk away with the grand in Crewe? One couple impress with their Georgian house. But after the pairs give their review. Who will be crowned the best cooking spouse? #WorldPoetryDay
Nathan loves music and his home town, but will his plans make his bride frown? He's got his theme, it's Northern Soul. But can Beccie cope with no control? #WorldPoetryDay
Hopeless romantic Gavin proposed on a hill, but was it all that much of a thrill? He's hoping to pull off a Thai-themed wedding, but is Somerset the ideal setting? #WorldPoetryDay
Time for bride Lauren to be in the dark, as groom Courtney sets their wedding at a park. But not just any park will do, this one's filled with caravans too! #WorldPoetryDay
Are Jas and Bobby heading for a scuffle, in this Titanic-style wedding kerfuffle? Bobby really wants to set sail; but does Jas think his plans are a fail? #WorldPoetryDay
Groom Micky may well be love-struck, but is he maybe pushing his luck? While Sophia wants a wedding that's grand, Micky's plans are more winter wonderland. #WorldPoetryDay
Stephen's undercover in Diamond Resorts International. But is his disguise a wee bit irrational? Before he begins the company probe, he decides he should pierce an ear lobe. #WorldPoetryDay
In PostNet we go undercover. But what will their CEO uncover? It's time for co-founder Steve, to see what they can achieve. But post-recession, can they recover? #WorldPoetryDay
Gordon Ramsay takes on Town's Inn. Where they've apparently never heard of a bin. There's hygiene issues. Please give them some tissues. And then the cleaning can begin. #WorldPoetryDay
Welcome to Lakeview Hotel, Lake Chelan; where owner Brent is without a plan. The restaurant and parlour are seriously failing. Cue Gordon Ramsay, will he be availing? #WorldPoetryDay
Verindar runs her place like a tyrant. Her employees are fed up with staying silent. So Gordon tries to sprinkle his magic. Hopefully the hotel won't stay so tragic. #WorldPoetryDay
If you're looking for a place to sleep, at this resort you won't get a peep. In these not-so-great digs, Brian hosts late-night gigs. And his service would make you weep. #WorldPoetryDay
Gordon visits a huge castle, where the scenery certainly dazzles. But sadly this citadel's no fairy tale; it's Hotel Hell on a massive scale. #WorldPoetryDay
Welcome to the comedy countdown quiz. With our resident wordsmith and maths whizz. Katherine Parkinson and Judi Love are guests. Putting their countdown skills to the test. #WorldPoetryDay
...Christmas Special: Let's look back on Christmas Day. When Jon teams up with Daisy May. Judi and Rob are agog. Joe brings the eggnog. And Jimmy rides in a sleigh. #WorldPoetryDay
How to enhance a one-man show? Add inventiveness and a musical flow. Well, we've got the comedy key. He's weird, he's wonderful, he's Bill Bailey #WorldPoetryDay
The Entertainer: Series two starts with Fran, ever keen to distract herself, taking up piano. Ever keen to delude himself, Bernard falls in love. All Manny wants is a break.
Fever: Fran can't sleep in the heat. Bernard needs a girlfriend to stop him staring. And Manny can't say what will happen if the temperature gets to 88 degrees.
Compilation show of the comedy panel game from 2012, with host Jimmy Carr, team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson and an array of celebrity guests.
Host Jimmy Carr and team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson return in the comedy panel game. Guests in this first show are Joe Wilkinson, Ellie Taylor, Natalie Cassidy and Gabby Logan.
The Entertainer: Series two starts with Fran, ever keen to distract herself, taking up piano. Ever keen to delude himself, Bernard falls in love. All Manny wants is a break.
Teleshopping
Teleshopping
The Hot Tub One: While bride-to-be Hayley always dreamed of a traditional Jewish celebration, bodylicious Ben would prefer a hot tub wedding. Will Hayley take the plunge?
The One with All the Pixies: Sam has £13k to spend on his pixie wedding to his love, Luanne. Will Luanne, who dreams of a traditional service, appreciate his scheme?
The One with the Dinosaurs: Elle has her heart set on a classy wedding at Liverpool's iconic Liver Building. But Phil plans a big day to go down in history.
Lorne Abony, the CEO and chairman of in-store media provider Mood Media, one of the fastest-growing companies in America, adopts a disguise to go undercover in his own business.
Jim Rogers, the chairman and CEO of Kampgrounds of America, the largest campsite organisation in the world, goes undercover in his business, disguised as an accountant.
There are bad table manners, disagreements about gender roles and spoonfuls of criticism - oh, and some British, European and Asian food too - in this episode from Somerset.
There are Thai classics, revelations, dancing and problematic experimental desserts as three couples compete in and around Bristol.
In East Anglia, Paul and Jill plan an Indian buffet inspired by their trips abroad. Lovebirds Tom and Bonnie serve a breakfast-themed bonanza. And gym bunnies Neil and Emma get competitive.
In East Anglia, Nikki and Clive host their first-ever dinner party, Lloyd and Molly serve up Thai treats, and Pete and Kirsty host with a letter P theme - but is it perfect or painful?
Peter's: Gordon visits Peter's Italian Restaurant in New York, where the peace of Long Island is shattered by the antics of the volatile Pellegrino family who run the place.
The Mixing Bowl: Bijou New York diner The Mixing Bowl is haemorrhaging money thanks to manager Mike's out-of-control marketing plans. Can Gordon get things back on track?
Seascape: Gordon Ramsay visits the Seascape restaurant in the seaside village of Islip, on Long Island in New York, where owner Peter has nearly a million dollars of debt.
The Secret Garden: Gordon Ramsay visits a romantic French restaurant in California, where temperamental chef Michel Bardavid's complicated cuisine is keeping the customers away.
Lela's: In California, Gordon Ramsay visits Lela's, a fine dining restaurant run by inexperienced owner Lela and young chef Ricky. Can Gordon save them from complete disaster?
Jimmy Carr, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host. Jon Richardson and Roisin Conaty take on Rob Beckett and Kiell Smith-Bynoe. Lou Wall joins Susie in Dictionary Corner.
The funniest bearded, babbling, bug-eyed druid the world has ever seen, captured in his natural habitat: the Hammersmith Apollo, during his sold-out comedy tour.
The Fixer: Manny uses his underworld connections to find Fran mysterious employment.  In return Bernard and Manny just have to educate a psychopath.
